articles.title: PM 'very worried' about Iran war escalation

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says he is "very worried" about fresh US strikes on Iran and has highlighted the difficulties for global markets responding to US President Donald Trump's repeated peace deal announcements.

image.altText: A man standing at a lectern flanked by four flags.

children.content: Iran says it struck the US Navy's Fifth Fleet base in Bahrain with drones following a series of US strikes on Iranian cities along the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

items.title: Iran attacks US navy base after US retaliates over helicopter downing

updates.title: US hit almost 20 targets in Iran, official says
Iran targets US air base in Kuwait
Iran targets US military base in Jordan

items.title: Sea drone saves US helicopter crew downed in waters near Strait of Hormuz

children.content: A look at the US Corsair sea drone used in a first of its kind mission to rescue two helicopter crew shot down near the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: A sea drone that looks like a high-tech speed boat skimming across the water.

items.title: Health authorities launch crackdown on illegal peptides, amid surge in use

children.content: The Therapeutic Goods Administration has launched a crackdown on unregulated peptides, saying increased illegal imports were posing a risk to consumers.

image.altText: A dermatologist checks the arm of a woman

items.title: High Court decision could lead to tens of millions of dollars worth of immigration claims

children.content: There are warnings a High Court ruling allowing a man to sue for false imprisonment over his indefinite immigration detention will lead to more claims running into the tens of millions of dollars.

image.altText: A square building behind a sign saying "High Court of Australia".

items.title: Woolworths to offshore hundreds of corporate jobs

children.content: Supermarket giant Woolworths will begin consultations with potentially affected staff today after announcing plans to shift some operations offshore.

image.altText: Close up of a Woolworths store entry sign with the green border and green W logo.

items.title: 'When Neale spoke, people felt it': Daniher farewelled at MCG funeral

children.content: Family, friends, members of the AFL community and dignitaries are attending the service to remember the AFL legend, MND campaigner and former Australia of the Year.

gallery.altText: A dark haired man with a beard leans over and kisses a coffin draped in the Australian flag with native flowers on top.
The crowd in the MCG stands at Neale Daniher's funeral wears Big Freeze beanies
A painting of a man between the Australian and Aboriginal flag above a coffin draped in the Australian flag and native flowers.

items.title: Bishop alleges 'coercive threats', 'overreach' by university regulator in resignation letter

children.content: The former ANU chancellor has accused the university regulator of "grievously" constraining her ability to do her role in a withering resignation letter tabled in parliament.

image.altText: A woman looks seriously in business attire.

items.title: Judge throws out appeals over homophobic tweet judgement

children.content: A Federal Court judge has dismissed an appeal against a judgement which ordered New South Wales MP Mark Latham to pay $140,000 to independent MP Alex Greenwich over a homophobic tweet in 2023.

image.altText: Mark Latham wearing a suit speaking to media on the street outside court.

items.title: Bear attack in Japan car park captured on CCTV as cities record more and more wild encounters

children.content: Bear encounters are rising in Japan, and the problem is not confined to the wilderness.

gallery.altText: A black-furred bear walks by a chain-link fence near a building.

thumbnail.altText: A black-furred bear walks by a chain-link fence near a building.

items.title: Military officers behind acid attack on Indonesian activist sentenced

children.content: Four military intelligence officers are sentenced to up to three years in jail, after they threw sulphuric acid over a human rights activist in a brazen attack in Central Jakarta.

image.altText: Four men dressed in military fatigues line up in front of a judge in Jakarta

items.title: Footage shows Mongol bikie's dramatic dog squad arrest outside Darwin

children.content: Northern Territory Police have released dramatic body-worn footage of the arrest of Mongols bikie Brandt Graham after the 44-year-old jumped a court dock last week, sparking a five-day manhunt.

image.altText: Nold_GrahamArrest_1006

items.title: When does the FIFA World Cup start and who are the favourites?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is the biggest single-sport event on the planet and has delivered numerous historical moments, from Diego Maradona's "hand of God" in 1986 to Zinedine Zidane's infamous headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

articles.title: Schwarzer confident 'written off' Socceroos can stun American doubters

children.content: Maligned, underestimated and overlooked — and that is just the way we like it according to the Socceroos' most capped player.

image.altText: Two men on a panel show, in a stadium, holding microphones

articles.title: FIFA prioritising profit over fans at World Cup, ex-official says

children.content: A former FIFA official says the upcoming World Cup has been tainted by corporate greed as two US states investigate ticket pricing and practices.

image.altText: Wearing a soccer jersey, Miguel Medina sits by a soccer field

articles.title: Will you be in North America for the FIFA World Cup? We want to hear from you

children.content: If you're an Aussie fan making the trip, or already there, share your experience with us.

image.altText: Generic 2026 FIFA World Cup football

items.title: Nant whisky founder Keith Batt to enter months-long fraud trial

children.content: Tasmanian whisky distillery owner Keith Batt is charged with more than 700 criminal offences, ranging from fraud, to stealing, to dishonestly obtaining a financial advantage.

image.altText: Keith Batt

items.title: The proposed NDIS cuts advocates worry could put women at risk

children.content: Planned cuts to NDIS community participation budgets could leave participants in unsafe environments, including increasing their risk of domestic violence, advocates have warned.

image.altText: A middle-aged white woman sitting in a wheelchair

items.title: Hayley's husband escalated control during her pregnancy. Her story is not uncommon

children.content: Pregnancy is a time of greater autonomy and self-awareness for women. Because domestic violence is about power and control, a pregnancy can challenge that for a person who is using abuse.

illustration of woman holding her baby in bed

thumbnail.altText: illustration of woman holding her pregnant belly new version
illustration of woman holding her baby in bed

items.title: Finding and saving the world's biggest trees before they disappear

children.content: It took a decade for researchers to find the tallest tree in East Asia. Now they know where the 84.1-metre conifer is, but the future of its species and other tall trees is dire.

image.altText: Man in orange jumper and climbing ropes prays with tree through hugging

items.title: Calling all budding storytellers living with disability

children.content: If you are an emerging content maker or journalist with disability and have a story to tell, now is your chance to work with the ABC to tell it.

image.altText: two women stand alongside one another smiling. One of the women has her right arm amputated. They stand in an ABC studio.

items.title: Why was Rolf Harris never tried for his crimes in Australia?

children.content: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

gallery.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

thumbnail.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

items.title: 'A pile of crap': Premier's mea culpa over Ogilvie scandal

children.content: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff says he "dropped the ball" regarding the allegations against a former minister, likening it to a "pile of crap" he has to clean up from "time to time".

image.altText: a man in a blue shirt and navy jacket and grey hair stands in front of plans

items.title: Musk's xAI hit with class action over power plant's 'inescapable' noise

children.content: US residents say a plant powering data centres has caused them emotional distress and reduced the value of their properties.

items.title: How the relocation of a Bunnings store ignited a furore in this town

children.content: A plan by hardware giant Bunnings move its Bunbury store 10km to a neighbouring shire has angered and concerned residents and businesses.

image.altText: The exterior of a large Bunnings Warehouse.

children.content: A new biopesticide could help battle growing resistance to varroa mite treatments in Australia.

items.title: Protests erupt in Belfast after stabbing attack, homes, cars and bus set on fire

children.content: Protesters hold anti-immigration demonstrations in Northern Ireland and England after a Sudanese man is arrested over a "brutal" knife attack in Belfast.

items.title: Why Xi's visit to North Korea is a flex for a new world order

children.content: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un's bilateral talks in North Korea were a big departure from the Chinese president's usual diplomacy playbook because, for this first time this year, he was the one travelling abroad to make the meeting happen.

image.altText: Two men wearing suits seen sitting and smiling in the crowd at a stadium.
